name: simplex-displacement
summary: A normal-driven displacement shader built around MIT simplex noise code.
description: A ShaderBase surface and geometry reference shader that displaces vertices along their normals using 3D simplex noise. The noise implementation is adapted from ashima/webgl-noise and wrapped in a simpler material contract for Three.js and React Three Fiber.

uniforms.name: uAmplitude
uniforms.description: Maximum offset distance along the mesh normal.

uniforms.name: uFrequency
uniforms.description: Spatial scale of the simplex noise field.

uniforms.name: uSpeed
uniforms.description: Rate at which the noise field animates over time.

uniforms.name: uLowColor
uniforms.description: Base color used in lower displaced regions.

uniforms.name: uHighColor
uniforms.description: Accent color used in higher displaced regions.

inputs.name: normal
inputs.description: Mesh vertex normals used to push the surface outward.

inputs.name: time
inputs.description: Elapsed time value supplied by the host application.

outputs.name: surfaceOffset
outputs.description: Animated displacement along the interpolated vertex normal.

outputs.name: surfaceColor
outputs.description: Color blend derived from the signed noise value.

recipes.summary: Create a ShaderMaterial and update its time uniform in a render loop.

placeholders.name: frequency
placeholders.description: Spatial scale of the simplex field.

recipes.summary: Use the shader as a reusable React Three Fiber material component.

placeholders.name: frequency
placeholders.description: Spatial scale of the simplex field.

sources.name: noise3D.glsl
sources.copyrightNotice: Copyright (C) 2011 Ashima Arts. All rights reserved.
sources.notes: The simplex noise helper was adapted into the vertex shader and wrapped in an original ShaderBase displacement material.

attribution.summary: Uses MIT simplex noise code from ashima/webgl-noise with an original ShaderBase displacement wrapper.
attribution.requiredNotice: Includes adapted simplex noise code by Ian McEwan / Ashima Arts, maintained by Stefan Gustavson, under the MIT License.

provenance.notes: ShaderBase authored the displacement controls, color blend, and integration recipes.
